explored_directions.description: Stitch Deep Integration — Stitch MCP as core design engine, NoPilot as thin orchestration layer. All UI generation, variants, Design DNA extraction through Stitch API. Tier 2 uses Stitch designMd philosophy to constrain agent HTML quality.
explored_directions.differentiator: Standing on Gemini 3.1 Pro design quality, NoPilot focuses on workflow orchestration only.
explored_directions.risk: Strong dependency on Stitch API — if Google changes API, limits quota, or MCP server maintenance lags, core feature breaks.
explored_directions.abandonment_reason: Vendor lock-in risk. User requires open adapter architecture (Direction 2).

explored_directions.description: Platform-Agnostic Preview Engine — Standalone design preview/selection engine consuming designs from any source (Stitch, Figma MCP, agent-generated, user uploads) through adapters. Preview, device simulation, selection callback, Design Token export are core infra.
explored_directions.differentiator: Not locked to any single design tool. Future-proof for Figma AI, v0, etc.
explored_directions.risk: Engineering cost doubled — each adapter needs individual development/maintenance. Only 2 providers initially, adapter abstraction may be premature.

explored_directions.description: Prompt-First Lightweight — Minimal infra: enhance discover prompt with UI taste questions, use Stitch when available but user reviews on Stitch website directly. NoPilot only calls Stitch API + extracts Design DNA + writes to discover.json. Reuse existing /visualize infra.
explored_directions.risk: UX discontinuity — user must switch between CLI and Stitch website. Selection callback is manual. Device preview depends on Stitch's own capabilities. Tier 2 degradation experience is poor.
explored_directions.abandonment_reason: User explicitly prioritized experience completeness over delivery speed. Context-switching violates 'zero-friction closed loop' philosophy.

explored_directions.description: Interactive Design Workshop (Design Co-pilot) — Full interactive design session in discover phase. Stitch generates initial variants, user iterates via text feedback, screenshot annotation, hybrid selection. Local preview with side-by-side comparison, device simulation, dark/light toggle. Auto-learning user preferences across iterations.
explored_directions.differentiator: Most complete experience — no competitor offers interactive design co-creation in requirements discovery phase.
explored_directions.risk: Largest engineering scope (9 phases, local HTTP server, selection callback, iteration history, font patching, asset inlining, three-tier degradation). May need 2-3 iterations to stabilize.

decision_log.detail: Merged Direction 2 (open adapters) + Direction 4 (design co-pilot) into 'Open Design Co-pilot'. User explicitly chose maximum experience completeness + vendor independence.
All 12 features kept in MVP. Zero cuts, zero deferrals. User confirmed 'all keep'.

12 requirements locked with EARS criteria. 3 high-risk items (REQ-005 DNA synthesis, REQ-006 source code parsing coverage, REQ-010-AC-5 Tier 2 quality) all resolved as ACCEPT_COST. 6 system invariants extracted. All 3 core scenarios covered.
